title: Can someone tell me what pins are used

messages.author: steve fox
messages.text: it is fixed, they are defined in the FW but I cannot find out what pins those are
messages.content_html: it is fixed, they are defined in the FW but I cannot find out what pins those are
messages.reply_html:
Lim Jahey
The wsl3 uses a esp32s3 Looking up the docs on that MCU, i2c can be set on any GPIO pin, it's not fixed to certain pins. Maybe that's why there's some discrepancies?

messages.author: Lim Jahey
messages.text: Ah I see, thery may not be defined for the WSL3, looking at the heltec v3 variant platformio.ini, all of the WSL3 sub variants don't have sda and scl defined. Only the V3 does. You could potentially get the to come alive (and use the ones as defined in proper WSL3 firmware by changing the highlighted flags as in the picture, and flashing using platformio. Note that the pins defined in the picture are just an example, I don't know which pins are exactly used. (edited)
messages.content_html: Ah I see, thery may not be defined for the WSL3, looking at the heltec v3 variant platformio.ini, all of the WSL3 sub variants don't have sda and scl defined. Only the V3 does. You could potentially get the to come alive (and use the ones as defined in proper WSL3 firmware by changing the highlighted flags as in the picture, and flashing using platformio. Note that the pins defined in the picture are just an example, I don't know which pins are exactly used. (edited)
